text.title: I Tried Living in Uluwatu, Bali as a Digital Nomad
text.u: Everybody comes to Uluatu for a holiday,
but what if you would actually live
here? I decided to find out what life is
really like here in Uluatu. Not just the
beaches and sunsets, but a proper
routine working here and staying healthy
and see if it could actually be the best
place to live. In this video, I will
show you the full experience, the villa,
the food, the gym, the traffic, and the
overall vibe and lifestyle here. So, by
the end, you will know if Uluatu could
be the perfect place to live long-term
for you.
[Music]
I came here to Nyangyang Beach to start
the video because I thought it would be
less touristy, less busy, more raw
nature vibe. And I was right. It's so
beautiful. Well, look at this. There's
no one around. Basically, the whole
beach. I just seen some people who
clearly live in Uluatu having a morning
walk, some local fishermen, but
otherwise there's like no one here. I
walked all the way to the other side of
the beach. Is so nice for morning walks
actually. Not so good for running
because the sand is quite uh like you
will sink into it a little bit, but uh
yeah, such a beautiful morning walk
spot. There's also someone walking from
the hill. You can drive a scooter, but
going it by food uh to have a more
exercise. So yeah, it's it's beautiful.
This is life. It's not even my favorite
beach, but it's still super nice. I
could get used to this, to be honest.
And there's even a couple of guys
surfing. It looks incredible. It's not
crowded at all. What a beautiful spot to
surf.
[Music]
[Music]
I'm back in my villa. I got a proper one
this time to see how it is to actually
live here if you would have a nice home
like this. And yeah, this villa is so
beautiful. I will talk more about it
later. I'm just going to get some work
done. And look how fast the Wi-Fi is.
So, it's really not a problem in if
you're in a nice place like this.
[Music]
In Jangu, my daily routine is to order a
double protein smoothie. Every single
day I do that and it's really important
part of my routine kind of. So I wanted
to see if I can do the same well here in
Uluatu because in Jangu the protein
places are just so good. But I found
this place that was pretty like it looks
really good. The it's huge. It's
actually very heavy in my hand. Like
that's a lot of protein smoothie. So,
let's see how it is. Wow, that's
actually insane. That's really good.
Just peanut butter, banana, oat with
double vanilla protein. And yeah, it was
uh on discount, so it's actually pretty
affordable. I came to check out the
nicest gym they have in Olawat. And it's
insane how busy it is here. Like so many
people in the cafe. The gym also looks
pretty busy. Not that crazy. There's
some glass going on.
Looks nice though. But what I came to
check out and try today is the new
recovery area they recently built. It
looks so nice here. There are three
different temperatures, ice beds, huge
jacuzzi, and of course the big pool area
which is really beautiful and vibe. It's
a proper olato vibe and a big sauna. So
that's super super nice. There's even a
little jungle gym and yeah, it's a bit
pricey. It's $265
for the gym and classes and the recovery
area for a month. But yeah, it's just
super super nice. But I would say it's
probably a little more busy here than in
the other gyms. There's actually so many
options nowadays for gyms and recover in
Uluatu. So there's lot to choose from.
So it's really nice. Actually, I didn't
realize that even in the sauna like
there's literally no one. It's just the
sun beds that are super busy
and the ice beds also not too busy
and really nice. Wow. Oh, feels good. I
feel like I would be pretty happy to
come here like monthly if I live in
Ulata. Like it's a really nice place.
But it's good to have other options
also. Also if this gets too busy can
change.
[Music]
I came to watch sunset in the closest
cliff spot from my villa which is this
famous cliff with incredible views.
Well, I guess this is the money shot
nowadays from this cliff. Wow.
So beautiful in Luat. So nice to come
watch sunset by the cliffs and beaches
every day. It's pretty crazy to have
this spot like just for myself here.
Like what a beautiful place. And by the
way, if you're living the normal
lifestyle, you know that having a
reliable mobile data is nonnegotiable.
And what I use for that is today's
sponsor, Olafly. And Olafly now has two
options. First, their classic eims.
Perfect for short trips or holidays. You
can set them up before your flight and
be online the moment you land in over
200 destinations. But if you travel
full-time like I do, their new Olafly
plans are a total game changer. It's one
eim that keeps you connected in 160
countries with monthly global data plan
and even a regular phone number you can
use for verifications and banking. No
matter where you're traveling or living,
it's the maximum convenience. No SIM
card swaps, no roaming fees, no more
running out of data. You just choose
your plan once, install it, and you are
covered everywhere. Honestly, it's the
kind of freedom every nomad dreams of. I
will leave a link in the description.
Trust me, it just makes travel so much
more easy. And with my discount code,
Yos the Nomad, you will get 5% off and
help support my channel.
[Music]
I came to the cashew tree for lunch.
Such a nice vibe and the food also looks
very good and healthy. Salad bowl and a
protein smoothie. Actually, I have found
so many restaurants that have healthy
food with enough protein and really
good. So, it's really not a problem
anymore in Uluatu when it comes to food.
It's really good here also. So, yeah,
for long-term living, it's all good
nowadays. Happy to see. I came to
Dreamland Beach to check out the surf as
it's definitely something that I would
want to do actively if I live in Oluatu
as it's famous for one of the best surf
scenes in the world. And what I can see
that here and all the beginner friendly
spots like Padang Padang, it's quite uh
crowded. So yeah, it's can be really
good place to surf, but there's a lot of
people surfing. But you can find those
more remote spots if you are more
advanced, I guess. But for me, not being
so good at surfing, not sure if it would
be the best here with the crowds. And I
asked the price to rent a board here. So
it's $6 per hour. So basically double
the price that it would be in Changu.
All right. So nice always off the beach
or from anywhere. Coming back home to
this just so peaceful. My little own
oasis here. Oh, this is life. It's
really theatu lifestyle. You can by the
way stay in this villa also yourself.
It's owned by one guy in my community
and uh yeah, it's pretty like tactical
location. So it's basically next to the
golf course road that goes to Dreamland
Beach and Bingin. So it's really easy to
drive down there. And there's also many
restaurants and gym in this area very
close by and also you closer to my
favorite beach, Melasti Beach, which I
will go later. And speaking of my
community, the Nomad Tribe, check it out
if you come to Bali or Southeast Asia
when I live the digital nomad lifestyle
or just for a few months. You will get
all the resources you would ever need to
have the best possible time and avoid
any mistakes and meet like-minded people
in the community group chat and meetups.
So, hope to see you inside. Link in the
description for this one also.
[Music]
I came to Island Paddle for a tournament
because it seems like the open games are
still a little bit hard to find here,
but the tournaments are happening every
week and they always fully booked. So,
seem like there's good community for the
tournaments at least. So, let's see how
it goes.
[Music]
Oh my god, it's so hot. But the
tournament was pretty nice. Like, it's
crazy that they play this time of the
day, midday, so it's extremely hot, a
little bit uncomfortable, but it's
pretty nice community vibe. Like,
there's a lot of people that clearly
play here often. And uh yeah, I would
say if I live in Ulwatu, it's still hard
to find open games, but coming here like
every week for the tournaments could be
pretty nice. So not as nice as in
Changangu, but it's okay. It's nice.
Uluatu is not known for working, but I
came to check this one co-working cafe.
Looks pretty nice here. I got some snack
and coconut. The Wi-Fi is not the
fastest ever, but uh it's decent. And
this sofa is really comfy. No need to
pay anything extra. just order food and
you can enjoy this nice view and get
some work done. There's also this room
with AC that is like a proper more
proper co-working but it has 250,000
minimum spend so you need to spend more
to stay full day here. And yeah, about
the traffic, it's pretty bad in Olvatu
nowadays. Like all the main roads,
there's insane amount of traffic. It's
really not relaxing to drive the main
roads, but at the same time, there's a
lot of the smaller roads that are very
peaceful, still not much going on. So,
it really depends where you go, but I
would say that in most cases, you're
going to be quite often in these roads
that get stuck a little bit and with
like crazy traffic jams every day. So,
it's definitely not optimal. Like for a
short stay, it's pretty manageable. It's
okay. But to live here long term, it's a
little bit similar than Ubud is that
it's a bit too much. Like I'm not sure
if I would actually enjoy so much. But I
guess if I would live here, I would just
avoid certain places and certain ways so
I don't need to go to the traffic too
often.
I came to Melasti Beach for swim and
chill. Is so beautiful. Look at this
water. It's really feels like Maldives
almost. It's incredible. There's no
doubts about Olivato being so nice for
the beaches. Like it's just so beautiful
and relaxing. Like that's something that
I definitely miss when I live in
Changoo. What a beautiful place to call
home. I mean would be a beautiful place
to call home. Like the sunsets, the
vibe, the beaches are just incredible. I
kind of feel bad about not living here
when I come here. But at the same time,
somehow it just never really feels like
home still as Changu does for me. But at
the same time, maybe I should give it a
chance. But I also noticed that being
here alone, it's not really it's kind of
feels weird. But being here with a
girlfriend, it feels good. So I feel
like it's it's a little bit this kind of
place that is not as good alone as Jungu
is. But if you have a partner, it can be
pretty perfect to spend a lot of time
here, go to the beach, chilling and
going to the restaurants, like it can be
really nice. But at the same time, the
traffic is a bit too much. So I'm not
sure if uh longterm I would feel good
here. But yeah, staying in a perfect
area, it could be nice. So yeah, I would
say if you enjoy this kind of place, I
would give it a try. But I'm not fully
convinced. So yeah, hope you got value
out of the video. Please like the video,
subscribe, and hope to see you inside
the Nomad tribe.
